profile.behavior: Usually forages alone on the ground, walking over open patches and probing soil with its long bill for insects and larvae. Flight is fast and undulating with deep wingbeats; the crest is often raised after landing. Nests in cavities.
field_marks: Warm cinnamon-buff head, neck, and breast
Tall erectile cinnamon crest with black tips
Very long, slender, downcurved dark bill
Broad, rounded wings boldly barred black and white
Black tail crossed by a broad white band
Dark eye and short gray-brown legs
profile.habitat: Semiopen country with bare or lightly vegetated ground for feeding and nearby cavities for nesting, including grassland, heath, farmland, orchards, savanna, wooded steppe, lawns, and forest clearings.

findings: The primary specimen is anatomically coherent: one head, one bill, two wings with the far wing naturally occluded, two legs, and one tail. The separate lower head and wing are clearly presented as detail studies, not extra appendages.

Minor anatomical stylization: the eye is somewhat oversized, while the fully raised crest, tail, and folded-wing barring are slightly exaggerated relative to the reference photographs. These do not obscure identification.

The cinnamon-buff plumage, black-tipped erectile crest, long slender downcurved bill, bold black-and-white wings, dark tail with a broad white band, dark eye, and short gray-brown legs agree with Common Hoopoe.

Common name, scientific name, family, measurements, habitat, behavior, field marks, and palette are accurate. The stated 25–32 cm length, 44–48 cm wingspan, and 46–89 g weight match the verified species account.
The centimeter ruler is decorative or ambiguous because it is not aligned with the diagonal bill-to-tail axis; it should not be interpreted as a precise scale measurement.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.
